title: Pollo en Mole Chicken Mole Sauce

seo.title: Authentic Chicken Mole Recipe (Pollo en Mole)
seo.metaDescription: Discover how to make authentic Chicken Mole (Pollo en Mole) with our step-by-step recipe. Rich, complex, and incredibly flavorful – perfect for dinner!
longTailKeywords: pollo en mole recipe step by step
seo.h1: Authentic Chicken Mole Recipe (Pollo en Mole)
h2Headings: Pro Tips for the Best Chicken Mole
Serving Suggestions for Pollo en Mole
seo.imageAlt: Authentic Chicken Mole (Pollo en Mole) garnished with sesame seeds and cilantro, served with rice.
seo.imageCaption: Savor the rich, complex flavors of homemade Authentic Chicken Mole, a traditional Mexican dish.

description: Pollo en Mole is a majestic Mexican dish featuring tender chicken bathed in a rich, complex, and deeply flavorful sauce. This traditional mole offers a symphony of tastes, blending chilies, spices, nuts, and a hint of chocolate for an unforgettable culinary experience. It is a true celebration of Mexican heritage, perfect for special occasions or a comforting family meal.

instructions.title: Prepare the Chicken and Chilies
instructions.text: Pat chicken pieces dry and season generously with salt. In a large pot, cover the dried Ancho, Mulato, and Pasilla chilies with hot water and let them rehydrate for 20-30 minutes until they are soft and pliable. Drain the chilies, reserving a cup of the soaking water, and remove any remaining seeds or tough bits from the chilies.

instructions.title: Toast Aromatics and Spices
instructions.text: In a large skillet or comal over medium heat, add 1 tablespoon of vegetable oil. Toast the almonds, peanuts, sesame seeds, and pumpkin seeds until they are fragrant and lightly golden, about 3-5 minutes. Remove and set aside. Add another 1 tablespoon of oil to the skillet. Sauté the chopped Roma tomatoes, tomatillos, white onion, and garlic cloves until they are softened and lightly browned, about 8-10 minutes. Add the rehydrated chilies, ground cumin, ground coriander, cinnamon stick, whole cloves, and black peppercorns to the skillet and cook for another 2-3 minutes, stirring constantly to release their aromas.

instructions.title: Blend the Mole Paste
instructions.text: Carefully transfer the toasted nuts, seeds, and the sautéed chili-vegetable mixture to a blender. Add 1 cup of hot chicken broth. Blend until a very smooth, thick paste forms. You may need to add a little more of the reserved chili soaking water or chicken broth, one tablespoon at a time, to achieve a thick, pourable consistency for the mole paste.

instructions.title: Simmer the Sauce
instructions.text: In a large heavy-bottomed pot or Dutch oven, heat the remaining 1 tablespoon of vegetable oil over medium heat. Carefully pour the blended mole paste into the hot oil. Cook the sauce, stirring constantly with a wooden spoon, for about 10-15 minutes. This crucial step allows the sauce to darken, thicken significantly, and helps the oil separate slightly, indicating the flavors are deepening and melding beautifully.

instructions.title: Cook the Chicken in Mole
instructions.text: Add the remaining 3 cups of hot chicken broth to the mole sauce, stirring vigorously until well combined and smooth. Bring the sauce to a gentle simmer. Carefully add the seasoned chicken pieces to the simmering mole, ensuring they are mostly submerged in the rich sauce. Reduce the heat to low, cover the pot, and let it cook for 30-40 minutes, or until the chicken is cooked through, very tender, and easily pulls from the bone.

instructions.title: Finish and Serve
instructions.text: Once the chicken is tender, remove the pot from the heat. Stir in the Mexican chocolate until it is fully melted and incorporated into the sauce. Add the sugar and taste the mole, adjusting the seasoning with more salt and sugar as needed to achieve a balanced sweet, savory, and subtly spicy flavor profile. Serve the Pollo en Mole hot, garnished with extra sesame seeds and fresh cilantro, traditionally alongside Mexican rice and warm tortillas.

tips: Toasting the chilies, nuts, and spices properly before blending is key to developing the deep, complex flavors of an authentic mole sauce. Do not rush this step, as it greatly impacts the final taste.
For a richer and thicker mole, consider adding a piece of ripe plantain or a few animal crackers to the blending stage; these traditional additions help thicken the sauce and add subtle sweetness.

Mole sauce often tastes even better the next day as the flavors meld and deepen. Store leftover mole in an airtight container in the refrigerator for up to 3-4 days, or freeze for longer storage, up to 3 months.
article:
Pollo en Mole: A Culinary Journey into Mexican Chicken Recipes

Mexican cuisine is celebrated globally for its vibrant flavors, rich history, and incredible diversity. Among its many jewels, Pollo en Mole stands out as a dish of profound cultural significance and unparalleled taste. More than just a chicken dish, Pollo en Mole is an experience – a complex symphony of chilies, spices, nuts, seeds, and chocolate that results in a sauce so deeply flavorful, it’s often referred to as the "sauce of the gods."

Originating in pre-Hispanic times and evolving through colonial influences, mole represents the ultimate fusion of indigenous ingredients and European culinary techniques. It's a dish reserved for celebrations, holidays, and special family gatherings, embodying the heart and soul of Mexican hospitality. While the thought of making mole from scratch might seem daunting, the reward of a homemade Pollo en Mole is immeasurable. This article will guide you through the process, demystifying the steps to bring this iconic Mexican chicken recipe to your table. Prepare to embark on a culinary adventure that will transform your kitchen into a haven of authentic Mexican aromas and flavors.

## What is Chicken Mole?

At its core, "mole" (pronounced MOH-leh) is a generic term for a variety of rich sauces in Mexican cuisine, often made with a base of chilies, spices, and other ingredients. When most people refer to mole, they are typically thinking of Mole Poblano, the dark, complex sauce that is the star of Pollo en Mole. This particular mole is famous for its intricate blend of anywhere from 20 to over 30 ingredients, including several types of dried chilies, nuts (like almonds and peanuts), seeds (sesame, pumpkin), warm spices (cinnamon, cloves, anise), fruits (raisins), and, surprisingly to some, chocolate.

The chocolate in mole doesn't make the sauce sweet; instead, it adds depth, a subtle bitterness, and a beautiful richness that balances the heat from the chilies and the earthiness from the spices. Historically, mole was often prepared by grinding ingredients on a metate, a traditional Mesoamerican grinding stone, a testament to the labor of love involved. Today, blenders make the process more accessible, but the essence of patience and layering flavors remains. Mastering the classic Chicken Mole Recipe is a culinary achievement that connects you directly to centuries of Mexican tradition, offering a taste that is simultaneously sweet, savory, smoky, and spicy.

## Ingredients for Pollo en Mole

Crafting an authentic Pollo en Mole requires a thoughtful selection of ingredients. While the list may seem extensive, each component plays a crucial role in building the sauce's legendary complexity.

**For the Chicken:**
*   1 whole chicken (about 3-4 pounds), cut into pieces, or 6-8 bone-in, skin-on chicken thighs and drumsticks
*   1 onion, quartered
*   2-3 cloves garlic, smashed
*   Salt to taste
*   Water to cover

**For the Mole Sauce:**
*   **Dried Chilies:**
    *   4-5 Ancho chilies (for fruity, mild heat)
    *   3-4 Mulato chilies (for smoky, earthy notes)
    *   3-4 Pasilla chilies (for complex, slightly sweet heat)
*   **Aromatics:**
    *   1 medium white onion, roughly chopped
    *   4-5 cloves garlic
*   **Nuts & Seeds (toasted):**
    *   1/4 cup blanched almonds
    *   1/4 cup peanuts
    *   2 tablespoons sesame seeds (plus more for garnish)
    *   1/4 cup pumpkin seeds (pepitas)
*   **Spices:**
    *   1/2 teaspoon whole cumin seeds
    *   1/2 teaspoon whole coriander seeds
    *   3-4 whole cloves
    *   1 small stick Mexican cinnamon (or 1/2 teaspoon ground cinnamon)
    *   1/4 teaspoon anise seeds (optional)
*   **Thickeners/Enrichers:**
    *   2 tablespoons raisins
    *   2-3 pieces of stale tortilla or a slice of bread (toasted or fried)
*   **Chocolate:**
    *   1-2 ounces Mexican chocolate (like Ibarra or Abuelita), or unsweetened dark chocolate (70% cocoa or higher)
*   **Fats:**
    *   2-3 tablespoons lard or vegetable oil
*   **Liquids:**
    *   4-6 cups reserved chicken broth (from cooking chicken)
*   **Seasoning:**
    *   Salt and sugar to taste (sugar helps balance the flavors)

## Step-by-Step Instructions for Chicken Mole

Creating an exquisite Chicken Mole Recipe is a process that rewards patience and attention to detail. Follow these steps for an authentic and delicious result:

**1. Prepare the Chicken:**
Place chicken pieces, quartered onion, smashed garlic, and salt in a large pot. Cover with water and bring to a boil. Reduce heat and simmer for 30-40 minutes, or until the chicken is cooked through and tender. Remove chicken and set aside. Strain and reserve at least 6 cups of chicken broth.

**2. Prepare the Chilies:**
Stem and deseed all the dried chilies. Heat a comal or dry skillet over medium heat and lightly toast the chilies for 15-30 seconds per side until fragrant (be careful not to burn them, as this will make them bitter). Immediately transfer the toasted chilies to a bowl and cover with hot reserved chicken broth or water. Let them rehydrate for 20-30 minutes until soft.

**3. Toast Nuts, Seeds, and Spices:**
In the same dry skillet, toast the almonds, peanuts, sesame seeds, and pumpkin seeds separately until lightly golden and fragrant. Remove and set aside. Lightly toast the cumin, coriander, cloves, cinnamon, and anise seeds (if using) for a few seconds until aromatic.

**4. Sauté Aromatics and Thickeners:**
In a separate skillet, heat 1 tablespoon of lard or oil. Fry the chopped onion and garlic until softened and slightly caramelized. Add the raisins and fry for another minute. If using, lightly fry the stale tortilla or bread until golden.

**5. Blend the Mole Paste:**
Drain the rehydrated chilies, reserving the soaking liquid. In a high-speed blender, combine the rehydrated chilies, toasted nuts, seeds, spices, sautéed onion, garlic, raisins, and fried tortilla/bread. Add about 2 cups of the reserved chicken broth and blend until completely smooth. You may need to do this in batches. For an extra-silky sauce, push the blended mixture through a fine-mesh sieve into a large bowl (this is optional but recommended for a truly refined mole).

**6. Cook the Mole Sauce:**
In a large, heavy-bottomed pot or Dutch oven, heat the remaining 2-3 tablespoons of lard or oil over medium heat. Carefully pour the mole paste into the hot fat. This step is crucial for developing the mole's deep flavor. Stir constantly for at least 15-20 minutes, allowing the paste to "fry" and darken slightly. It will thicken considerably. Gradually add the remaining chicken broth, one cup at a time, stirring continuously to incorporate. Bring the sauce to a gentle simmer. Add the Mexican chocolate and stir until it completely melts and is incorporated. Continue to simmer the mole for at least 30-60 minutes, stirring occasionally, allowing the flavors to meld and deepen. The sauce should be thick enough to coat the back of a spoon. Season with salt and a pinch of sugar (if needed) to balance the flavors.

**7. Combine Chicken and Mole:**
Add the cooked chicken pieces to the simmering mole sauce. Gently coat the chicken and continue to simmer for another 15-20 minutes, allowing the chicken to absorb the rich flavors of the mole. Following this detailed Chicken Mole Recipe will ensure a truly authentic dish.

## Pro Tips for the Best Chicken Mole

Making mole from scratch is a labor of love, and a few pro tips can elevate your homemade Chicken Mole Recipe from good to unforgettable:

*   **Patience is Paramount:** Do not rush any step, especially the toasting of ingredients and the simmering of the sauce. Each stage contributes to the final depth of flavor.
*   **Don't Burn the Chilies:** Toasting chilies briefly enhances their flavor, but burning them will make your mole bitter. Watch them closely and remove them from the heat immediately when they become fragrant.
*   **Toast Ingredients Separately:** Different ingredients toast at different rates. Toasting nuts, seeds, and spices individually ensures they are perfectly done without burning.
*   **Fry the Paste Thoroughly:** This is arguably the most critical step. Sautéing the blended mole paste in hot fat for an extended period "cooks out" the raw chili flavor and allows the complex flavors to develop and deepen. It will also prevent the mole from separating later.
*   **Blend Until Silky Smooth:** A powerful blender is a mole maker's best friend. For the smoothest sauce, blend thoroughly and consider straining the paste through a fine-mesh sieve.
*   **Taste and Adjust:** Mole is all about balance. Continuously taste your sauce as it simmers. Adjust salt, and don't be afraid to add a little sugar if the sauce seems bitter or needs a touch of sweetness to round out the flavors. A small splash of cider vinegar can also brighten it.
*   **Make Ahead:** Mole, like many complex stews, often tastes even better the next day as the flavors have more time to meld. With these pro tips, your homemade Chicken Mole Recipe will impress even the most discerning palates.

## Serving Suggestions for Pollo en Mole

Pollo en Mole is a hearty and flavorful dish that shines with simple accompaniments.
*   **Traditional:** Serve your mole-smothered chicken with a side of fluffy white rice, which helps to soak up the rich sauce.
*   **Tortillas:** Warm corn tortillas are essential for scooping up every last drop of the delectable mole.
*   **Garnishes:** A sprinkle of toasted sesame seeds adds texture and a nutty finish. Freshly chopped cilantro and thinly sliced red onion offer a bright counterpoint. A dollop of Mexican crema (or sour cream) can also provide a cooling contrast.
*   **Sides:** A simple green salad or refried beans can complete the meal.

## Variations and Substitutions

While the traditional Chicken Mole Recipe is a masterpiece, there are many ways to adapt it:

*   **Different Proteins:** Mole is incredibly versatile. It can be served with turkey (Pavo en Mole), pork shoulder, beef, or even vegetarian options like roasted cauliflower or hearty mushrooms.
*   **Other Moles:** Mexican cuisine boasts dozens of mole varieties, from the vibrant Mole Verde (green mole) made with tomatillos and fresh herbs, to the fruitier Manchamanteles (tablecloth stainer), and the slightly simpler Coloradito. Each offers a unique flavor profile.
*   **Shortcuts:** For those short on time, good quality jarred mole paste is available in many Latin American markets. While it won't replicate the depth of homemade, it offers a quick way to enjoy the flavors. You can also use pre-cooked rotisserie chicken.
*   **Chocolate Adjustment:** The amount of chocolate can be adjusted to your preference. For a richer, darker mole, use more chocolate; for a less pronounced chocolate flavor, use less. Always opt for Mexican chocolate or good quality unsweetened dark chocolate. Experimenting with a classic Chicken Mole Recipe can lead to delightful new family favorites.

Pollo en Mole is more than just a meal; it’s a journey through Mexican culinary history, a celebration of complex flavors, and a testament to the art of slow cooking. While the ingredient list may be long and the process calls for patience, the reward of creating this iconic dish from scratch is truly unparalleled. The rich, velvety sauce, infused with the essence of chilies, spices, nuts, and chocolate, perfectly complements tender chicken, creating an unforgettable experience. Whether you stick to the traditional or try a variation, a good Chicken Mole Recipe is a journey of flavor. This comprehensive guide to the Chicken Mole Recipe empowers you to create an unforgettable meal that will transport your taste buds straight to the heart of Mexico. Enjoy the process, savor the aromas, and delight in the incredible taste of your homemade Pollo en Mole.
